Satsang with Mooji
Everything has a cause, but the Lord is sarvakarana karanam, is the cause of all causes.
When there was nothing, He was there.
Sometimes people find it difficult to comprehend,
and a very commonly asked question, generally when you address somebody who is totally new,
to Krishna consciousness, who created God?
Okay, we can understand that God created everything, but who created God?
It's like that chicken and egg story.
Chicken comes from egg, and as it grows up, the hen gives egg.
So what came first? People are perplexed.
So one of the definitions or understanding of God is He is anadi.
He is not an ordinary mundane person.
Anadir adir govinda sarvakarana karanam.
He is the cause of all causes.
He is without beginning and is without an end, and He is the beginning of everyone.
So everything what we see in this creation,
has come from Him.
Although He is without beginning, but He is the beginning of everyone.
So then further Prabhupada mentions that,
although the Lord is a person, He is present everywhere.
He is perceivable everywhere, because He is self-effulgent.
This is again, very difficult for many people to comprehend.
This is again, very difficult for many people to comprehend.
Because all along we have an understanding of a person who is limited in time and space.
If you see a person in a particular place, you can very confidently conclude that He is not in another place.
But here it is said that the Lord is all-pervading.
If He is a person, how can He be all-pervading?
Because all persons whom we have seen are limited by time and space.
If they are present in place A, they cannot be simultaneously present in place B.
So here it is said that the Lord is present everywhere by His energies.
The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord.
The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord.
The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord.
Nothing but the Lord and His energies.
Energies, broadly speaking, material energy and spiritual energy.
The Lord is present anywhere and everywhere.
And He is present through the distribution of energy.
In the Bhagavad Gita also it is confirmed that the Lord is all-pervading
and exists everywhere by two kinds of energy, one material and other spiritual.
Both material and spiritual energies are spread everywhere
and that is a proof of existence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
So in the material world, we living entities who have rebelled against the Lord
are under the influence of material energy.
And this material energy, Krishna says,
deviyesha gunamayi mamamaya dhuratyah
This maya, material energy, which is composed of three modes of material nature,
binds the living entity, entangles the living entity.
And that knot is very, very stringent.
prakrte kriyamanani gunayi karmani sarvasah
We helplessly act under the influence of the modes of material nature which is influencing us.
Just like we see typically a rope made of three strands intertwined.
So likewise, these three gunas,
the influence of these three gunas act like a rope which binds the living entity.
If we see any printed picture,
we see books with color plates,
we see calendars,
we see posters,
magazines, brochures,
all very colorful these days.
The printing technology has advanced.
There was a time when a lot of things were done black and white.
It was very expensive to print in color.
But these days we see a lot of colorful prints.
Actually, if you go to a printer,
all these colorful prints,
all these beautiful calendars,
all colorful posters,
they are just made of...
anyone has an idea how many colors?
Four colors.
C, M, Y, K.
Cyan, magenta, black and yellow.
If we closely see the...
that's why we call it DPI,
dots per inch.
Higher the DPI,
you will not be able to see the dots.
It's so close,
so densely packed.
And different colors are produced by
the different size of dots
and how densely they are close to each other.
If you see a picture which is by and large reddish in color,
you'll have more of magenta dots
and densely populated.
If you see Radha Krishna calendar
and if you see dress of Radharani,
it's yellow in color.
If you closely see with a very powerful lens,
you will see it's made of yellow dots.
And if you see orange,
it is combination of yellow and magenta dots.
And human eye cannot perceive it.
So just like we have unlimited shades possible
by combination of three colors,
four colors,
likewise unlimited combinations of behaviors,
perspective of life,
consciousness is possible by the influence
of these three modes of material nature.
And we helplessly act under the influence of these modes.
We see many times our behavior changes
as we advance spiritually.
Many times our perspective of life changes.
Our understanding of world changes.
It is because of the modes which are influencing is changing.
If we are in tamo guna predominantly,
we'll have one perspective of life.
We'll have a particular kind of behavior.
In general, we will be lazy.
We will be clouded with different kinds of ignorance.
As we rise in the modes,
in rajo guna,
in tamo guna,
sorry,
in sattva guna,
our perspective changes.
That's why a symptom of sattva guna is enlightenment,
is knowledge.
Just like an example is given,
if you take an alkali,
we have all studied in chemistry,
alkali and acid.
If you take an alkali
and you combine with another alkali,
there is no reaction.
You take a powerful acid
and combine with another acid,
there is no reaction.
You take an alkali and an acid,
you mix together,
there is a powerful reaction.
Likewise,
a person who is influenced by tamo guna,
if he goes to a tamasic place,
will he feel out of place?
No.
He is comfortable.
There is no reaction.
If you take a person who is predominantly in sattva guna,
you take him to a place where tamo guna is predominant,
he will feel very uncomfortable.
He will feel like,
let me just get away from this place
as early as possible.
See, different behaviors
are all nothing but the combination of modes
which are influencing us.
Prabhupada used to say,
birds of same feather flock together.
Swans, you will find them in very clear water bodies,
nice landscapes, lakes.
They feel very comfortable there.
But you will not find many crows there in that place.
Crows are more comfortable where there is filth,
where there is some kind of a garbage,
where there is some kind of dead corpse.
So they enjoy that.
Likewise, in this material world also,
we see people have different inclinations,
different behaviors.
They are comfortable with different things.
Some people are very comfortable
with where there is dirt all around.
Some people don't take bath for days together.
They are comfortable with that.
Can you see how the modes of material nature
which influence us,
conditions us,
binds us,
and makes us behave in a particular way?
In Mumbai,
we have a place where fruits are available near IIT market.
And we have to pass through a place
where they also sell,
it's called fish market.
And it smells so bad,
so bad.
Most of us cannot pass through that place.
So whenever we have to go to buy fruits,
we take an additional half a kilometer detour
and reach that place.
And whenever we are going,
we sometimes talk among ourselves,
how do people tolerate this smell?
But if you see there,
some people are very,
very comfortable there,
nicely living there,
because they've got condition.
The modes influencing them
has conditioned them
and that has become a new normal for them.
So a person who is in sattva guna,
who is influenced by spiritual energy,
he'll feel very,
very uncomfortable
where material energy is very predominant.
Tzu,
this is in my heart.
Okay,
so I walked a little bit
and I was very comfortable
when I saw these Cafe,
where I was very comfortable.
So,
once I walked,
I was very comfortable.
By the way,
I went to the cafe,
and there was a very good time for the dessert.
So I had a good time there.
I was very comfortable,
there was no smell,
the drinks were very good,
and really,
people had so much fun.
So,
the best things for the dessert
is having good time.
So,
So they'll put all that in a jar and that jar was left one place in the house for days together, for months together and every time you keep on adding something which is left over of a non-veg food.

Because that food rots for days together, months together, it ferments and it produces some kind of a liquid and on special occasions, on festival days, they stain that liquid and they share with each other and that delicacy is called naafi.
So when devotees heard that, they were completely, how can people eat like this?
And one day it so happened, there was Sunday feast and they were cooking puris made of pure ghee and this neighbor started complaining, what kind of smell is this?
It was intolerable ghee, as if you know, so that's the meaning of how the modes influence us.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something sattva guna is done, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something sattva guna is done, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something sattva guna is done, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable.
he will feel uncomfortable.
Tamo guna, tamo guna, no reaction.
So from this also we can understand
that if we are in a place
which is predominated by tamo guna
and we don't feel uncomfortable
that means we are in tamo guna.
Two days, assuming
somebody is not taken bath,
he is very comfortable with that, he has not changed
his dress, worn a jeans for
seven days without washing,
typically in hostels they save water.
Washing happens once in a month.
So these are all symptoms of tamo guna.
So this material energy
which is comprised of these three modes
which binds the living entity
which forces him to act
in a particular way,
three guna
which Krishna talks about
daivi, esha, guna, mai, mama, maaya,
dhurateya
and the best part is the living entity
is unaware that he is under the influence
of these modes. Forget about getting out of it.
Here the Lord is mentioned as
transcendental to the material
modes, his shuddha
sattva.
So when we touch the Lord,
when we come in contact with the Lord,
when we chant the names of the Lord,
when we touch that supreme
personality of Godhead who is
supremely pure,
who is transcendental to the material modes,
we also gradually elevate
in the modes and transcend the modes.
The example is given
just like you take
an iron rod, put it in fire,
it becomes hot. Keep it in fire,
it becomes warmer.
And when time comes, it becomes red hot.
It acts like fire.
So likewise
the Lord who is all spiritual,
when we who are under the influence
of material energy,
come in contact with the Lord
by the process which is prescribed by the
acharyas, we slowly
come out of
the modes of material nature.
We transcend the modes and come
to shuddha sattva.
But unfortunately
because we are conditioned,
there is a natural
inclination for us to fall
back. Material
energy is very lucrative for us.
It's very tempting for us.
The allurement,
sense objects, it's very alluring
which pulls us down,
pulls our consciousness down.
So that's why
genuine spirituality involves
on one side some do's
like chanting, reading,
hearing, participating in kirtans
which elevates our consciousness
and some don'ts which
generally pulls the consciousness
down.
And this material
energy which binds the living entity
actually
prahupada here mentions
the living entity
which is under the influence of this modes
of material nature
is battered
by this material energy
by three kinds
of kleshas.
Actually battered.
We have
Three kleshas, adhyatmik kleshas, adhidevik kleshas, adhibhautik kleshas.
Miseries due to our body and mind, miseries due to other living entities, and miseries due to natural calamities.
And in spite of being battered by the material modes, deviesha gunamai, mamamaya dhuratya.
Dhuratya means it is very difficult for the living entities, struggling.
But because we are under the influence of lower modes, we are not able to comprehend that.
We still are too eager to exert our supremacy, that yes, I am in control.
I don't need the Lord. I can manage my life on my own.
I can maintain an existence independent of the Lord.
Whereas Krishna says, mamamaya dhuratya, mam eva ye prapadyante.
Mam eva ye prapadyante.
If you surrender to me, these modes of material nature which are working under my direction, under my supervision, it is my energy, I will relieve you from these modes.
One time there was a lion in the jungle.
So generally said to be the king.
So he wanted to just reinforce his supremacy as being the king of the jungle.
So one day he happened to see a monkey in the jungle.
So he went to the monkey and said, hey monkey.
So the monkey was shaken and growling.
The lion said, hey monkey.
He says, who is the king of the jungle?
So in a very shaky voice, in a timid voice, the monkey said, you sir, you sir, you are the king.
So he was quite happy with that answer.
He said, don't you forget.
He said, don't you forget.
He said, don't you forget.
So then he started walking away.
There he saw a zebra.
So again he wanted to double check, reinforce his supremacy.
He said, hey zebra.
This time he rode.
And he said, who is the king of the jungle?
So zebra again with fear, body shaking, he says, you sir, you.
You are the king of the jungle.
He says, don't you forget that.
Kept moving.
And then he came across an elephant.
Huge, gigantic elephant.
So again, as usual, rolled and growled.
He says, hey elephant, who is the king of the jungle?
So elephant didn't reply.
He took him in the trunk and threw him against the tree.
In comics you come across, you know, when a person gets this thing, he sees stars and all this.
So, you know, he got smashed against a tree.
Then this elephant walked towards this lion.
Stepped on his tail.
Again picked him up and smashed him on the ground.
And started walking away.
So the lion got up.
And he said, why are you getting angry if you don't know the right answer?
After having got smashed also, he is not willing to give up the idea that he is not the king of the jungle.
So, if we are in tamo guna, if we are not blessed with divya gyan, if we don't have transmittal knowledge,
when the material energy batters a living entity,
he is not willing to give up
that I am subordinate to the Supreme Lord.
The Lord is supreme. I am amsa.
I am a small amsa of the Lord.
That's why Krishna says,
veviesha gunamai mamamaya dhurateya
And he also says,
bhau naam janma naam ante gyanvaan mam prapadyante vasudevam sarvam iti samayam.
bhau naam janma naam ante gyanvaan mam prapadyante vasudevam sarvam iti samayam.
bhau naam janma naam ante gyanvaan mam prapadyante vasudevam sarvam iti samayam.
bhau naam janma naam ante gyanvaan mam prapadyante vasudevam sarvam iti samayam.
but that living entity, after many many...
after getting battered, we learn some things.
A person who has some sense will learn from something.
Although we are 3rd class intelligent
we dont even learn sometimes.
This material energy
is designed in such a way so that
Even the difficulties, even the clashes
it is all sanctioned by the Lord
for our benefit.
Just like, sometime doctor gives an injection.
Sometime doctor may prescribe a diet which is bitter.
That's why Krishna says,
I am the well-wisher of all living entities.
So if we are blessed with divya jnana,
if we have a mercy of a pure devotee of the Lord,
then even when we go through some kleshas,
it's a very big learning for us.
Miseries can teach us many lessons which otherwise we don't learn.
That's why in this material world,
we many times are complacent.
We have relationship.
We think these relationships are permanent,
but they come to an end.
The Lord wants to teach us that this is a temporary place.
It's not a place where we can permanently settle down peacefully.
There's another world and he's inviting us to that world,
which is permanent, which is eternal.
He is supreme.
Sita is a material world.
There's nothing more that can teach us.
There's nothing there.
Sita is a material world.
There's nothing there.
Sita is a material world.
There's nothing there.
So this modes of material nature,
which is binding the living entity,
which is like the ropes,
it is said that in this material world,
just like in a prison house,
this material world is also compared to a prison house.
In a prison house,
you have a jail superintendent.
Likewise,
the Lord's representative,
Durga Devi,
Durga Devi is in charge of this material world, which is also called Durga. Durga means fort.
Just like a person who is imprisoned in a fort, he cannot cross the walls, he cannot come out of the prison easily.
Durga Devi, it is said that she has ten hands. The ten hands represent the ten directions.
North, east, south, west, north-east, south-east, south-west, north-west, top and bottom.
Imagine we are in a room which has ten CCTV cameras.
Top all four directions.
Top, down, left, right.
Can we do something which is, which a person who is monitoring the CCTV cameras, we can avoid him being aware of?
No.
If it is one CCTV camera, maybe you can take a particular position and you can still not be visible in that camera.
So likewise, that's why Krishna says,
devī eṣa guṇamāyī mamamāyā dhuratyā.
We are in the clutches of this material energy.
And the Lord, who is the creator of this material world,
He is the supreme controller.
He is the cause of all causes.
He is the supreme cause, the super-soul of everything.
When we surrender unto Him,
then He relieves us from the influence of this modes, this material energy.
So we should have a context of what we are doing.
Many times when we chant, when we do our sadhana, when we do our reading,
we should know what are we doing.
Like yesterday, we heard in a class,
it's energy management.
There is material energy, there is spiritual energy.
And we are called the marginal energy.
Sometimes we are predominantly under the influence of material energy.
Sometimes under the influence of spiritual energy.
So when we chant, when we engage in spiritual activities,
we invite the spiritual energy to act on us.
We come under the influence of spiritual energy.
And under the influence of spiritual energy,
we elevate in the modes and transcend the modes.
And when we transcend the modes,
try to transcend the modes.
And when we transcend the modes,
then the living entity who is Sat Chit Ananda,
who is eternal, Chit, knowledgeable, Ananda, blissful,
he realizes his true constitutional position.
He achieves what he is hanging on to.
What he is anchoring, what he is desiring,
what he is longing to achieve.
And that is pure, transcendental, eternal happiness
in association with the Lord.
We stop here.
Krantaraja Srimad Bhagavatam ki Shila Prabhupada ki
